title: Backend query filtering by caller/principal for all node types

requirements.text: Modify the backend Motoko actor to add or update the graph data query so that it accepts the caller's principal (via the implicit `caller` context in a shared query) and returns only those nodes — Locations, LawTokens, InterpretationTokens, Curations, and Swarms — where the stored creator/owner field matches the caller's principal.

quotes: getgraphdata should only show those nodes where the caller/principal is the creator

acceptanceCriteria: The query function uses `msg.caller` (or equivalent caller principal) to filter results.
All five node types (Locations, LawTokens, InterpretationTokens, Curations, Swarms) are filtered so only nodes whose creator field equals the caller's principal are returned.
An anonymous caller receives an empty result set for each node type.
Existing node data structure and stable state are not altered — only the query return logic is changed.

requirements.text: Update `getGraphData()` in both `frontend/src/pages/GraphView.tsx` and `frontend/src/pages/TreeView.tsx` to call the updated backend query that filters by caller/principal, replacing the previous unfiltered call. Ensure the React Query cache key reflects the authenticated principal so the data re-fetches correctly when the user logs in or out.

quotes: update getGraphData() in both GraphView.tsx and TreeView.tsx to use the filtered query

acceptanceCriteria: `getGraphData()` calls the new filtered backend query instead of the old unfiltered one.
When the user is logged in, only nodes they created are displayed in both GraphView and TreeView.
When the user is not logged in (anonymous), the graph renders empty or shows an appropriate empty-state message.
The React Query cache key includes the caller's principal so data refreshes on login/logout transitions.
No runtime errors occur when the backend returns empty arrays for any node type.

constraints: Do not alter the persistent data schema or stable state variables in the Motoko backend.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/ (immutable paths).
The filtering must be enforced on the backend (caller-side principal check in Motoko), not solely on the frontend.
nonGoals: Filtering by any criterion other than creator/principal (e.g., date, tags, swarm membership).
Adding new node types or changing existing data models.
Changing the visual design or layout of GraphView or TreeView.
Modifying the TerminalPage, FilterSortModal, or any other component that also uses graph data.
